_doc: TrainingPeaks static-plan SKU families — northstar P3.1. Each family × duration (8/12/16wk) = one TP marketplace plan, built once in TP's plan builder from the generated manifest. Race→family maps live in each brand repo at data/tp-sku-map.json; TP URLs get pasted into data/tp-sku-links.json there. Design center: 6-10 hrs/week, intermediate athlete (FTP 230 / 165 lbs).

gravel-ultra.name: Gravel Ultra-Distance Plan
gravel-ultra.tagline: For 150-mile-plus gravel: durability is the event.
gravel-ultra.notes: Built for ultra-distance gravel (150-200+ miles, 12+ hour days). Durability is the defining demand: long rides are the centerpiece, fueling is trained like an interval, and late-race resilience efforts simulate hour ten, not hour one.

gravel-climber.name: Gravel Climbing Plan
gravel-climber.tagline: For mountain gravel: sustained climbs decide the day.
gravel-climber.notes: Built for climbing-heavy gravel races (8,000ft+ over 100 miles). Sustained climbing strength: low-cadence muscular work, threshold repeats, and long rides with real elevation. Includes altitude arrival guidance for high-elevation events.

gravel-conditions.name: Gravel Heat & Altitude Plan
gravel-conditions.tagline: For races where the weather is the main rival.
gravel-conditions.notes: Built for hot or high gravel races where conditions decide outcomes. Standard race fitness plus a dedicated heat-acclimatization block in the final 3 weeks, sweat-rate-aware fueling progression, and conservative pacing protocols for the first hour.

gravel-fast-punchy.name: Fast Gravel Plan
gravel-fast-punchy.tagline: For short, violent gravel: speed and surges.
gravel-fast-punchy.notes: Built for shorter, fast gravel races (40-75 miles) decided by repeated hard surges and group dynamics. VO2 development is the spine; race simulations rehearse attacking and recovering at race pace. Handling skills under fatigue included.

gravel-allrounder.name: Gravel Race Plan
gravel-allrounder.tagline: The standard gravel century build, done right.
gravel-allrounder.notes: Built for the classic gravel century: a balanced demand profile where threshold durability, fueling execution, and pacing discipline beat any single superpower. The plan most gravel racers actually need.

road-alpine-fondo.name: Alpine Gran Fondo Plan
road-alpine-fondo.tagline: For the big mountain fondos: Maratona, Marmotte, Otztaler.
road-alpine-fondo.notes: Built for alpine gran fondos with 10,000ft+ of climbing. Sustained climbing power at threshold, gearing-realistic low-cadence strength, descending recovery skills, and altitude arrival strategy. The long ride progressively rehearses back-to-back climbs.

road-rolling-fast.name: Fast Road Race Plan
road-rolling-fast.tagline: For fast, rolling road races: speed wins, surges decide.
road-rolling-fast.notes: Built for fast rolling road races and fondos decided by group riding and repeated accelerations. VO2 intervals are the spine, threshold supports, and race simulations train surge-and-settle rhythm — the skill of going hard twice.

road-distance.name: Road Endurance Plan
road-distance.tagline: For double centuries and audax-length days.
road-distance.notes: Built for long-distance road events (130+ miles). Durability-first: progressive long rides, fueling trained at volume, position comfort, and the steady-state engine that makes hour eight feel like hour three.

road-allrounder.name: Gran Fondo Plan
road-allrounder.tagline: The standard gran fondo build, done right.
road-allrounder.notes: Built for the classic gran fondo: balanced demands where threshold fitness, group-riding economy, and fueling execution decide your day. The plan most fondo riders actually need.
